attacking_style: Exploits GC freedom when 8-10+ minutes down, targeting breakaways on mountain stages. Capable of sustained late-race accelerations in final kilometers to distance fading climbers.
team_tactics: BORA deploys him in stage-hunting mode rather than pure GC defense, targeting bonus seconds and breakaway opportunities when overall classification is out of reach.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages where climbing form allows exploitation of breakaway scenarios. Strong enough to survive selection races and finish ahead of fading GC contenders.
vulnerability: Unable to sustain GC position across multi-week Grand Tours—loses significant time mid-race, forcing shift from classification to stage-hunting tactics.
key_patterns: Transitions from GC fade to dangerous breakaway rider within same Grand Tour (2020 Giro evidence).
Tagged as primary threat by GC teams when 10 minutes down, indicating climbing caliber (2020 Giro pattern).
Finishes strong in final kilometers against fading climbers when in hunting mode (2019 Vuelta, 2020 Giro).
Team pivots to opportunistic stage tactics after GC hopes fade mid-race (2020 Giro multiple stages).
form_trajectory: neutral: 2020 Giro showed strong climbing legs in breakaways despite earlier GC fade, suggesting reliable stage-hunting form but inconsistent week-three GC sustainability
